query: 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary status: Byron Donalds candidacy, opponents, Trump endorsement, polling, competitors dropping out. Who is running against Donalds and what are the polls showing as of mid-2026?
answer:
Research complete. Here's a summary of the key findings on the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary as of mid-July 2026:

**Byron Donalds — Dominant Frontrunner**
- U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds (R-Naples) is the runaway leader with President Trump's endorsement (since Feb 2025).
- $90M+ raised, far outpacing all rivals.
- Early July 2026: swept up major DeSantis-world and GOP establishment endorsements — AG James Uthmeier, Senate President Ben Albritton, and incoming legislative leaders Jim Boyd & Sam Garrison — even though Gov. DeSantis himself has endorsed no one.

**Opponents Still Running (field did NOT clear)**
- **Jay Collins** (Lt. Gov., DeSantis appointee) — refuses to drop out, but DeSantis hasn't endorsed him.
- **James Fishback** — facing a ballot-eligibility/residency challenge; a court hearing on Collins's lawsuit to remove him was set to begin July 21, 2026.
- **Paul Renner** — former House Speaker, low single digits.
- No major candidate has dropped out. Casey DeSantis never entered.

**Polling (GOP primary)**
- Emerson (Mar): Donalds 46%, Collins 4%, Fishback 4%.
- AIF (Jun 3–7): Donalds 54%, Fishback 8%, Collins 5%, Renner 2%.
- Donalds's lead has run ~39–46 points in public polls. The only close result (37%–20% over Collins) was a Collins internal poll.
- Polymarket: Donalds ~95.8% to win the primary (July 12, 2026).

**Bottom line:** Donalds is a near-certain nominee, dominating on endorsements, fundraising, and polling, while his opponents remain in the race but increasingly fight each other rather than close the gap. Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been submitted.

key_facts: Republican primary is Aug 18, 2026; general election Nov 3, 2026; Gov. Ron DeSantis is term-limited and cannot run.
Byron Donalds is the clear GOP frontrunner with President Trump's endorsement (backed since Feb 2025).
Donalds has raised $90M+ total for the race, far ahead of all rivals.
Early July 2026: DeSantis allies and GOP leaders endorsed Donalds—AG James Uthmeier, Senate President Ben Albritton, incoming Senate President Jim Boyd, incoming House Speaker Sam Garrison.
Gov. DeSantis has NOT endorsed anyone, including his own appointee Lt. Gov. Jay Collins.
Main opponents still running: Lt. Gov. Jay Collins, James Fishback, and former House Speaker Paul Renner.
No major candidate had dropped out as of mid-July 2026; Collins repeatedly refuses to exit.
Collins filed suit to remove Fishback from the ballot over residency/eligibility; a two-day hearing was set to begin July 21, 2026.
Casey DeSantis did not enter the race; when tested in a March poll she drew only ~7%.
AIF poll (Jun 3-7, 2026): Donalds 54%, Fishback 8%, Collins 5%, Renner 2%, undecided 31%.
A Collins campaign internal poll (July 2026) showed the closest result: Donalds 37%, Collins 20%.
Polymarket priced Donalds at ~95.8% to win the GOP primary as of July 12, 2026.
